title: Substack vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Substack and Constant Contact. Learn pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your needs best.
h1: Substack vs Constant Contact: A Complete Comparison
intro: Substack and Constant Contact serve different segments of the email marketing landscape. Substack is designed for individual writers and creators monetizing direct relationships with subscribers, while Constant Contact caters to small businesses, nonprofits, and organizations needing comprehensive marketing tools. Understanding their distinct approaches will help you choose the right platform for your goals.

comparison_table.tool_a: Free with revenue-share on paid subscriptions
Paid subscriptions, podcast hosting, notes feed, built-in audience
comparison_table.tool_b: Event management, social media ads, SMS, e-commerce integration

choose_a_if: You're an individual creator or writer wanting to monetize a newsletter directly
You want to start for free with no subscriber limits
You prefer a platform with built-in audience discovery and recommendations
choose_b_if: You're a business needing SMS, social media ads, and e-commerce capabilities
You require event management and advanced list-building tools
You want affiliate commission opportunities ($80 per paid signup)

faq.q: Is Substack better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: Neither is objectively better—it depends on your use case. Substack excels for individual creators monetizing content, while Constant Contact is superior for businesses needing integrated marketing tools like SMS, social ads, and e-commerce. Constant Contact offers more comprehensive business features, whereas Substack provides a simpler, creator-focused experience.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Substack or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Substack is cheaper if you're starting out—it's completely free with unlimited subscribers. Constant Contact requires payment starting at $12/month. However, Substack takes a revenue cut from paid subscriptions, so costs may increase as you monetize; Constant Contact's fixed pricing may be more economical for established businesses with large subscriber bases.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to Substack?
faq.a: Yes, you can export your email list from Constant Contact and import it into Substack. However, the platforms serve different purposes—Substack is optimized for monetized newsletters while Constant Contact handles broader marketing campaigns. The switch is technically possible but may require restructuring your content and strategy to align with Substack's creator-focused approach.
